idea-plugin.name: EntropyGuard
idea-plugin.description:
EntropyGuard — AI Code Entropy Detection

Detect structural disorder in your codebase before it compounds. EntropyGuard analyzes in-progress code changes in real-time to identify how AI-generated code introduces hidden entropy—structural disorder that increases over time without obvious failure.

Why EntropyGuard?

AI-generated code is not broken—it gradually increases system entropy. EntropyGuard detects this hidden structural degradation, helping developers maintain codebase health and prevent technical debt from compounding silently.

Detection Categories

The plugin detects entropy across 5 major categories with 15 sub-categories:

1. Complexity (8 rules)

  • Long methods (>50 lines)
  • Deep nesting (>3 levels)
  • High cyclomatic complexity (>10)
  • Heavy boolean logic (>3 operators)
  • Long if/else-if chains (>2 branches)
  • Large class state (>8 fields)
  • Null propagation patterns (.orElse(null))
  • Excessive null returns

2. Duplication (4 rules)

  • Duplicate string literals (>2 occurrences)
  • Duplicate number literals (>2 occurrences)
  • Repeated method calls (>3 duplicates)
  • Large method signatures (>4 parameters)

3. Performance (2 rules)

  • Thread.sleep calls
  • Magic number usage (>3 occurrences)

4. Security (10 rules)

  • Null assertion (!! operator) — HIGH severity
  • Broad exception catch
  • Empty catch blocks
  • Plaintext password comparison
  • Hardcoded API tokens (AWS, GitHub, Google, Stripe, JWT, Bearer, Basic auth)
  • Placeholder domains (example.com, localhost, etc.)
  • Hardcoded configuration (URLs, passwords, endpoints)
  • Broad exception catch (AST)
  • Empty catch block (AST)
  • Hardcoded secrets (AST)

5. Corruption (4 rules) — New in v1.1.0

  • Parse failure (both JavaParser + Tree-sitter fail)
  • Markdown tokens (code fences, file markers)
  • Unbalanced braces (>2)
  • Mixed language content (>30% prose density)

Additional Sub-Categories (entropy-based detection)

  • Boilerplate Bloat (getter/setter ratio, avg method length)
  • Verbose Comment Spam (comment-to-code ratio)
  • Over-Defensive Programming (null check density)
  • Poor Naming (single-letter identifiers)
  • Framework Misuse (exception swallowing patterns)
  • Excessive Documentation (javadoc-to-method ratio)

Key Features

  • - Real-time Entropy Detection - Analyze code changes as you type
  • - Entropy Scoring - Get instant entropy scores (0-100) per category
  • - Security Detection - Identify potential security vulnerabilities
  • - Code Quality Insights - Receive suggestions for code improvements
  • - Inline Feedback - See issues directly in your editor
  • - Comprehensive Reports - Detailed analysis with up to 7 prioritized findings
  • - Performance Analysis - Detect performance bottlenecks
  • - Best Practices - Ensure adherence to coding standards

Supported Technologies

  • Languages: Java (full AST analysis), Kotlin, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript (diff-based)
  • Frameworks: Spring, React, Angular, Vue.js, Django, Flask
  • Build Tools: Maven, Gradle, npm, yarn, pip
  • IDE Integration: IntelliJ IDEA 2023.3+

Getting Started

  1. Install: Install from JetBrains Marketplace
  2. Auto-analysis: Plugin analyzes code changes automatically
  3. View Results: Check the EntropyGuard tool window
  4. Review Findings: Click issues to see details and jump to source

Technical Stack

  • Java Parser: Full AST analysis for Java
  • Tree-sitter: Fuzzy duplicate detection + multi-language support
  • Kotlin + kotlinx.serialization + SnakeYAML
  • IntelliJ Platform SDK (243.*+)

Free vs Premium

  • Free: 7-day trial
  • Premium: Unlimited usage (requires license)

action.description: Run AI code risk analysis on the current file
Run comprehensive AI risk analysis on the entire project

idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.0 - 2026-04-30
  • Renamed plugin to EntropyGuard
  • Added detection of corrupted source files as a new top-level category
  • Improved detection of common AI duplication signals
  • Improved detection of common AI security anti-patterns
  • Got code generator tool 100% working
1.0.3 - 2026-04-25
  • Added new improved icon
  • Fixed licensing to correctly use IntelliJ Marketplace Trial API
  • Fixed code generator compilation issues
  • Added support for aichat, llm, and ollama CLI tools
1.0.2 - 2026-04-17
  • Disabled MCP server (temporarily)
  • Click to jump to problem files
  • UI layout improvements
1.0.1 - 2026-04-14
  • General improvements and bug fixes
1.0.0 - 2026-04-12
  • Initial release to IntelliJ Marketplace
  • Real-time code risk analysis
  • Security vulnerability detection
  • Code quality insights and suggestions
  • Inline editor feedback
  • Dedicated tool window for analysis results
